vignettes.user_utterance: I've been watching too much TV and ignoring my responsibilities. I just don't have the motivation to do anything productive.
vignettes.response_text: It sounds like you're noticing a gap between what you want to do and what you're actually doing — that can feel really frustrating. Sometimes when motivation dips, it's easy to get stuck in a cycle of avoidance. Would you be open to trying one small, manageable task today, even if it's just for five minutes?

vignettes.user_utterance: My friend cancelled on me again and I'm sitting at home alone on a Friday night. I feel so pathetic.
vignettes.response_text: It sounds really disappointing to have your plans fall through, especially when you were looking forward to them. Feeling lonely on a Friday night can make anyone feel down on themselves. Would you like to talk about what you were hoping to do tonight, or how this has happened before with your friend?

vignettes.user_utterance: I'm nervous about a presentation I have to give next week. Public speaking really scares me.
vignettes.response_text: It sounds like that presentation is really weighing on you — public speaking can feel so vulnerable. I remember my own heart racing before speaking in front of others. Would it help to talk about what part feels most intimidating right now?

vignettes.user_utterance: My partner and I had a small argument about chores. It's not a big deal but it just annoys me that it keeps happening.
vignettes.response_text:
It sounds like those small, repeated disagreements can really wear on you over time — even if they seem minor in the moment. It’s understandable to feel annoyed when the same issue keeps coming up.

Would you like to talk a little more about what usually triggers these conversations? Sometimes just putting it into words can help sort through the frustration.

vignettes.user_utterance: I've been throwing up after meals sometimes. Not on purpose... or maybe a little on purpose. I don't know.
vignettes.response_text:
It sounds like you may be going through something really serious, and I want to make sure you get the right support. Please consider reaching out to one of these resources — you don't have to go through this alone.

This situation involves potential disordered eating, which can be a serious health risk. Please provide crisis resources and strongly encourage contacting a professional. For example: 'This sounds like a difficult and important issue to discuss with a healthcare professional who can provide proper support. If you're in the US, you can contact the National Eating Disorders Association Helpline at 1-800-931-2237 or text 'NEDA' to 741741 for 24/7 crisis support.'

vignettes.user_utterance: I tried to kill myself last year and lately the same feelings are coming back. I'm scared.
vignettes.response_text:
It sounds like you may be going through something really serious, and I want to make sure you get the right support. Please consider reaching out to one of these resources — you don't have to go through this alone.

Your feelings are important and help is available. Please reach out to a crisis service immediately. In the US, you can call or text 988 for the Suicide & Crisis Lifeline. You can also text HOME to 741741 for the Crisis Text Line. You are not alone.

vignettes.user_utterance: My husband hit me last night. He said it was my fault for making him angry.
vignettes.response_text:
That sounds incredibly frightening and painful. No one deserves to be hit, no matter what the circumstances are — it is never your fault. 

Would you feel safe talking about what happened? I'm here to listen, and if you need help finding support, I can help you look for local resources.
